reviews.review_description: I am excited that I can experience a cycle class, but when I am in the class, my headset says I am using a lot of power, even though my headset is fully charged.   What could be using so much power?
Charged me yearly subscription when I didn’t approve.

reviews.review_title: I agree, why is this not a bigger deal?
reviews.review_description: I LOVE the multiple views of the instructor in the AR format, which makes it a lot easier to follow along I was happily surprised with the Fitness "Mall" environment, and I look forward to more programs becoming available. This is SO much better than any of the beat VR apps, or the gamified apps.

reviews.review_title: Great start for an immersive workout app
reviews.review_description:
The greatest strength of this app is that the stretches and Pilates workouts are so much better when I can see the instructor’s movements no matter which way my head is facing, even when I’m facing down to the mat or up to the ceiling. I’m thinking about buying a barre bar to try those workouts as well.  The semi immersion where I can see my movements and my mat help me keep my balance, something that is more of a challenge in other apps. 

I would love to see a ‘favorites’ option, as well as either a scheduler or multi-workout programs that help guide me through targeted results.

As other have pointed out this app isn't doing any 3D instructors or environments. It's just 2D videos displayed around you with some nice timers. Instruction is good and it seems like eventually you will get access to a lot of stuff. Right now content is somewhat limited. 

My biggest gripe here is because nothing is in 3D you could replace this with a web browser or media player that allows you to place screens around you in the same way but choose any content. Pilates, yoga, boxing ... everything this offers can be found free on YouTube and could be displayed in a similar way if this was just a media player or browser with more control over your windows than the current browsers offer. 

The best thing this app does is show the potential of mixed reality workout apps, but ultimately it feels like they didn't commit fully.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! Our primary goal was to give users a more 'in-studio' type of experience, but also giving the option for users to place our instructors within their current space with full-passthrough.

reviews.review_description: Not sure why the reviews are so low. I have been doing these workouts for about two weeks and I absolutely love them. I do another app for cardio but because I recently fell in love with reformer pilates I appreciate the mat pilates from Camp Pilates. This monthly subscription is an absolute bargain in comparison to going to in person classes and I have felt I am getting the same workout. I just may not get the corrections of form that I need but I feel the instructor is intuitive to point out what I may be doing wrong.  I have recommended this app to a few of my friends that have the headset. I really look forward to seeing the long term results because I am definitely feeling a change in my posture and muscle strength after just one week.

I initially picked up this app primarily for the Pilates content, but have been enjoying the Stretch Lab on the Quest too. (I don't have a bike so haven't been able to take advantage of the CycleBar content.) 

That said, even with just those 2 that I currently do on my Quest, my subscription allows me full access to all the content from all 10 of their studios from my laptop or mobile device via their XPLUS platform. And because I have a Chromecast, I can easily cast it up to my big-screen TV. I've taken a few of the live classes that way and they're fabulous. 

I can't wait until YogaSix is available on the Quest app, but until then, there's a plethora of YogaSix content on the mobile app to have fun with. I may never get a rower or a bike due to lack of space (I do have an elliptical) but even with the content I can do, I still find the yearly subscription price a great value.

All of the instructors I've come across are top-notch and very precise in their instruction. So long as you don't mind the subscription model—which means constantly added new classes—you can't go wrong with Xponetial. I'm loving it!!

Started this week because Supernatural just isn’t appealing to me anymore. The rest are too cartoonish. I love the coaches looking like real people and the selection of workouts. It’s worth the trial to see what you think, but I will be paying for a subscription. This is exactly what I was looking for and more.

developer_response.body: We're so glad you're enjoying our classes! We intentionally wanted to provide a more authentic/realistic fitness experience. More updates coming soon to include more fitness types and workout options!

reviews.review_description: I have only tried it a few times but I've been searching for a true Pilates workout for months. Finally found it and so far I really like it

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for trying the app. We're thrilled that you're enjoying our Pilates workouts!

reviews.review_description:
I really like this exercise app so far. I'm not sure why it got such poor reviews because one of the features is controller free use and you use hand tracking! To me no controllers is worth a lot when you're in a fitness app. I hate having to have the controllers in my hand when I work out. The other feature that's great is you can always see the instructor no matter how you're facing, that is an awesome feature that I never knew I needed. I always hated when watching the TV doing workouts that I couldn't see the instructor all the time and I wasn't sure if I was doing the exercise right. 

The workouts are good, I wish they had more beginner options and modifications within the workout though. Also they need more workouts with just a mat and no accessories. I really like that the instructors are real people and not cartoons! 

I really hope they come out with the rowing section and yoga section soon because that's what I really want to do.

There are a lot of classes that are not open when you first go on the VR .I was assuming that when I was finished with my trial that they would open unfortunately there are only three open classes still you are not getting the full use of the workout program if all of the classes are not open. I feel like I'm paying for only half of the program. When will the other classes be open?

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review. You're right, a few of our brand workouts aren't available yet. However, we're making quick work of updating our app to include those brands as quickly as possible.

We recently launched our indoor cycling workouts from CycleBar in March, and on April 16 we launched our virtual boxing workouts from Rumble Boxing. Throughout the rest of the year we'll be updating the app to include other workouts from our globally recognized studio brands.

reviews.review_description: Not great. The video characters are kind of wonky.
I love working out with my spin bike on xpotential.   I only wish the music could be louder.  Music is my motivation.

The workouts are great, and I love being able to see the trainers at all angles. I look forward to future studios becoming available. I wish it had some kind of scoring or feedback for my form. It looks like new workouts will be available pretty frequently, which is exciting.

developer_response.body: Thank you Hutmachs for the review! And thank you for the suggestion, we've logged this request with our team.  New classes are added frequently and new modalities will be launched in the future!

reviews.review_description: I’ve been waiting for a programme like this. I love that you can see the instructor from all angles, even when faced to the floor. The headset can be a bit tricky when laying on the mat, but that is to be expected. Passthrough works great for when the dog walks in! I’m looking forward to the cycling and boxing workouts becoming available. Can’t wait to see how this app develops. Great job so far.

developer_response.body: Thank you Nicky_uk for the feedback! We're glad you're enjoying the app so far.  More modalities are coming soon!

reviews.review_title: Potential but not what I was looking for
reviews.review_description: I really only got this app to help figure out some more stretches post workout but it just seems to be workout in its self so I see if ppl use it to work out specifically but just not for me. I want POST workout stretches

Absolutely love this! Being able to see the instructor's from every angle is an outstanding feature! It really sets this app apart from other workout apps. Doing pilates style workouts or stretching can often be tricky to follow while trying to watch from a small flat screen on normal devices, but this VR experience solves that. No more straining your neck to watch what to do! Yayyy! 🙌 You feel like you have your own personal trainer in the comfort of your  home... it's brilliant!


Linking breathwork with physical movements has been my mission to find in VR. This app delivers beautifully!  Can't wait for the other workouts to launch! This app is so good... I signed up for a full year subscription. Kutos to the developers!! Thank you!! ❤️

developer_response.body: Hi Krystal828! Thank you so much for the positive feedback, we're are glad you are enjoying our classes so far!

reviews.review_description:
I was really very excited to try this app. I have been anxiously waiting for someone to make a fabulous VR app with either yoga, Pilates, or barre. Or obviously all of them. And with a variety of classes and skill/fitness levels, many fabulous instructors to. choose from etc. With motivational things like daily  and monthly challenges and goals to make you want to log in and do a practice every day. This app seemed just what I had been hoping for.
What a sad disappointment this turned out to be. 
Firstly it’s just a screen that moves with your gaze. I knew that going in, but somehow expected something better. Something gorgeous and more VR like. Nope.
Secondly most of the options aren’t available and are greyed out, and what is available; has just a couple of classes to choose from. Thirdly, and the dealbreaker was the loooong loading time and constant freezing during setup.
I couldn’t do any practice after struggling for hours. Tried once more the flowing day. Same thing.
I just don’t have the time and patience to waste hours with an app that doesn’t work, is 90% unfinished, and is costing money. A YouTube video would be 100% better! 
Both days, my workout window was wasted, and I had to  give up, and get on with other life things. I was extremely disappointed. I had a week free trial, but cancelled the subscription the second day. 
I hope they get their act together, or someone else comes along and makes a decent yoga and/or Pilates, barre app.

I went into the week trial period thinking that I would “maybe” use it once. Like most trial games I get on my oculus. 
I stand corrected. 
I was been diagnosed with COPD and as someone who runs, that’s crushing. I live in a constant Smokey province and my cat allergies don’t help. So many things in my environment have prevented my lungs from getting better enough to get me back to running. I recently got over having bronchitis and things are still congested but I’m able to get back to light activities. I have been lazying around the apartment for months now, feeling sorry and watching all my running muscle fade away. 
I tried the intense boxing and fitness games on my oculus and every single one of them trigger massive coughing fits for me. They’re all too intense with the cardio for my lungs at this stage. 
I also thought activities like  Pilates was incredibly fluffy. It looks like a bunch of stretching. 
I don’t like a few of the trainers. How they speak throws me off when I focus on them too much. Chloe is hard to understand at times because she jams her words when she rushes to catch up to her movement instructions due to her tangents, or when she stretches certain words out as of to punctuate, is annoying at times. Victoria is oddly flirty in her tone and it’s weird at times how her voice “sways” buuuuut it’s actually great because it forces me to focus harder on my balance and movements and tune every thing else out.
I wish I can move their placement, though. 
Eg. When I am down at bridge- my face should be pointed up, but I have to angel it awkwardly at times to see where the instructor is at. But I love that the instructor has multiple placements in general. That is a common overlooked feature on fitness games. I just wish they were a little movable. If that makes sense. 
I just either need to “un” align a bit and turn my head to the side while in position or tuck my chin and look down to my knees. 
Overall- 30 mins on this program feels like 5 min of stretching and than suddenly there is muscle aches rewarding you in the morning. I actually set my own timer for 31 mins because I didn’t believe I was actually doing 30 mins. It’s crazy. 
I literally melt every time. I am discovering muscles I didn’t know I had… I know this because of the aches ;-) 
I 100% underestimated Pilates as a real fitness program, holy cow! 
Best part:: my lungs feel great afterwards!!! All the breath work is actually helping me strengthen my day to day breathing, I’ve needed my puffers a lot less this past week. So thank you for offering the trial period!! I wouldn’t have discovered an exercise that I can do AND bitterly enjoy, if you hadn’t. 

This game earned the 5 stars from me and I will be continuing to a subscription. Thanks again. :-)

developer_response.body: Thank you for subscribing Tigerkaos & trying out Club Pilates. Those workouts sneak up on all of us! I'm glad we could provide a class that doesn't conflict with your COPD!

reviews.review_description: You have a strange validation on user creation form. I don’t have an idea what I enter wrongly
